handoff.reason: Strict 5-phase reality-sync plan authored + validated; ready for phased execution with operator review gates between each surface.

execution_rules: strict plan_type: every phase is a hard stop — report back + await operator review before the next phase.
Two repos: Phases 1 land in CODEREF-CORE; Phases 2-3 land in ASSISTANT; Phases 4-5 land in CODEREF-CORE (reindex artifact + .mcp.json + WO artifacts).
Commit each repo by EXPLICIT path (main-only; never git add -A).
Phase 4 reindex is long-running and ollama-local — hard stop before it fires so the operator controls timing.
hard_constraints: LLM/embedding work is Ollama LOCAL-ONLY: no cloud API keys (operator directive 2026-07-02). Reindex uses ollama 768-dim; strip any inherited OPENAI_API_KEY ghost from the process first.
Do NOT bypass close-workorder hard gates (no --force; SKIP_COMMIT_CONTENT_CHECK forbidden).
Regenerate foundation-docs from the generator where possible rather than hand-patching (avoids re-drift).
Anchor all doc/skill claims to the shipped reality: close 4cd33af, baselines 2411 elements/257 files, graph 2771 nodes/29123 edges, 11 MCP tools, json vector store (sqlite = deprecated alias).

decisions_log.detail: ADJ-P2T2: P2-T2's 'also fix any fictional flags' clause surfaced extensive drift beyond the GAP-03 prefix fix. Verified vs live CLI sources: coderef-pipeline docs claimed --stages/--resume + pipeline-state.json resumability + legs 'scan,populate,validate' (REAL: --only/--skip/--dry-run/--rag-reset/--ollama-base-url/--ollama-model/-v; legs scan,populate,docs,rag; no resumability). coderef-rag-server docs claimed positional ops start|query|index|status|reset + --top-k/--query-timeout-ms (REAL: always-on HTTP server, 'coderef-rag-server [--port <n>]' only, port 52849, HTTP endpoints /api/rag/status|query|index). coderef-watch docs claimed positional <project_path> + --include/--notify + debounce 500 (REAL: --project-dir flag, --languages/--include-rag/--once/--no-pipeline/--json, debounce 30000). Operator approved full flag/invocation-surface rewrite to shipped reality this phase, same treatment as ADJ-P1T4/P1T5.

ADJ-P2T4: bare SKILL.md commit blocked by skill mutation-routing pre-commit hook (SKILL.md hand-edited without skill.json version bump -> Claude/Codex/agy wrappers + TRACKING/skills.json go stale). Also hit a phase-language validator FALSE POSITIVE on the word 'duration' in a CLI-output report field (reworded to 'elapsed timing', no bypass needed). Operator approved expanding P2-T4 commit scope beyond the 6 SKILL.md files to include the regenerated skill.json (x6) + surface wrappers + TRACKING/skills.json via /coderef-skill --update. Behavior-doc changes warrant the version bump. Pre-existing frontmatter-vs-skill.json version skew (rag-index SKILL.md 1.0.0 vs skill.json 1.1.0) noted, not introduced by this WO.

Superseded by the pre-execution /discover gaps@medium audit run earlier this session (folded 4 gaps). @medium is a strict superset of the @quick pre-phase check; not re-fanned to preserve spawn-budget.

P1-T1 verification found the guide-to-coderef-core.md deleted-symbol surface is larger and more live/stale-mixed than P1-T4's coarse description ('remove buildGraph/saveGraph/GraphError + error chapter'). Confirmed vs src/index.ts + src/parser + src/utils: DELETED = buildGraph, saveGraph, loadGraph, GraphError, GraphErrorCode, CodeRefError, ParseError, FileNotFoundError, ValidationError, IndexError, generateCodeRef, extractCodeRefs, and package-level `logger` (not re-exported from index). LIVE (must NOT delete) = scanCurrentElements, LANGUAGE_PATTERNS, ElementData, ScanError (scanner error-reporting module, different class), parseCodeRef, CanonicalGraphQuery/loadCanonicalGraph, buildDependencyGraph/DependencyGraph/GraphNode/GraphEdge, saveIndex. Spans ~15 refs L114-L1120. Halting for operator ruling on rewrite depth before editing the guide.

Ran scripts/doc-gen/* generators (all exit 0) + validate-docs (exit 0, 21 pre-existing UUID-anchor warnings only). Generator REBUILT INDEX.md/EXPORTS.md/HOTSPOTS.md/RELATIONSHIPS.md from the fresh .coderef/index.json (2411 elements) — those 4 are now clean; RELATIONSHIPS.md correctly shows 2771 nodes / 29123 edges. BUT the generator does NOT rebuild API.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, COMPONENTS.md, SCHEMA.md (hand-authored; ARCHITECTURE+SCHEMA only get 'enhanced', which does not strip stale content). Those 4 still document deleted AnalyzerService / QueryExecutor / GraphBuilder.buildGraph() as public API (~25 references: API.md L110/189/373-404, COMPONENTS.md L126/181/573/607/841, ARCHITECTURE.md L162/184/312/453/571/582/715, SCHEMA.md L454). P1-T5's fallback clause ('else hand-correct') applies. Halting for operator ruling on hand-correction depth for the 4 non-generated docs.

P1-T7 CHECK premise ('two open stubs carrying now-unsatisfiable analyzer-service.ts constraints') does not hold against the current registry. Searched ASSISTANT/tracking/stubs.json (90 stubs) + improvements.json + active-work: the ONLY analyzer-service references are (a) this WO's own parent STUB-GSDZ7X (status=promoted — describes the sync, not an analyzer-service fix constraint) and (b) this WO's own active-work entry. ZERO open/active stubs carry an analyzer-service.ts work-constraint. Nothing to supersede or annotate. Like GAP-04, a no-op CHECK here is SUCCESS, not a failure — fabricating stubs to annotate would be wrong. P1-T7 satisfied: no reconciliation needed.

ADJ-P1T5: hand-correct the 4 non-generated foundation-docs (API.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, COMPONENTS.md, SCHEMA.md) onto the canonical-graph query surface — same treatment as guide-to-coderef-core.md P1-T4. Generator-owned docs (INDEX/EXPORTS/HOTSPOTS/RELATIONSHIPS) already regenerated clean and left as-is. Operator-approved use of P1-T5's 'else hand-correct' fallback clause.

ADJ-P1T4: expand P1-T4 from the plan's coarse 'remove buildGraph/saveGraph/GraphError + error chapter' to a full precise rewrite — remove every confirmed-DELETED symbol and rebuild the graph-persistence + error-class chapters onto the live surface (buildDependencyGraph, CanonicalGraphQuery, scanner ScanError), preserving all live symbols. Operator-approved scope expansion.
Operator chose to fold RAG reindex + .mcp.json restore INTO the WO alongside docs/skills/CONTEXT.md sync.

decisions_log.note: graph.json still lists 3 deleted examples/plugins files (9 ENOENT during conversion, chunks dropped not written); fresh populate-coderef would clean graph — OUT of P4 scope (reindex-only)

decisions_log.detail: git checkout -- .mcp.json restored the tracked-but-deleted file (was ' D .mcp.json', last committed ef68c3c) to a clean working-tree state matching HEAD. Restored entry: mcpServers.coderef-core -> node dist/src/cli/coderef-mcp-server.js --project-dir C:/Users/willh/Desktop/CODEREF/CODEREF-CORE (absolute, cwd-independent). Re-establishes project-local registration, eliminating fallback to the global ~/.claude.json entry that resolves .coderef/ from session cwd.

Drove the coderef-mcp-server binary over stdio EXACTLY as .mcp.json invokes it (node coderef-mcp-server.js --project-dir <abs CODEREF-CORE>) from a FOREIGN cwd (C:/Users/willh/Desktop, deliberately not coderef-core) to prove cwd-independence. Result: server banner 'project: ...CODEREF-CORE (11 read-only tools)'; tools/list returned exactly 11 (codebase_summary,cycles,diff_impact,find_element,hotspots,impact_of,rag_search,validation_status,what_calls,what_exports,what_imports); codebase_summary returned project_dir=CODEREF-CORE, total_elements=2411, graph 2771 nodes/29123 edges — matches reality anchor (close 4cd33af). Baseline resolves from any cwd via --project-dir binding. NOTE: the live in-session MCP client does not hot-reload .mcp.json mid-session; the restored config takes effect on next client start — verified equivalence via direct binary invocation instead.

plan_adjustments.change: Corrected prescribed README edge-baseline value: was '29123 edges' (statistics.edgeCount); the target line uses valid_edge_count (reality anchor 5154). Prevented injecting a new falsehood by metric-conflation.
Added README.md:67 '6 read-only tools' -> 11 fix (previously fixed only in CONTEXT.md P3-T1; README copy was uncovered).
Reworded entrypoint fix: skills dropped the coderef- bin prefix (pipeline.js->coderef-pipeline.js etc., verified vs dist/src/cli), not phantom paths. Noted coderef-scan is the unprefixed exception.
Reclassified EDIT->CHECK: coderef-scan skill already correct (positional path + scan.js); verify-only regression guard, no-op = success.

communication_log.detail: Promoted STUB-GSDZ7X; authored context/analysis/plan/communication; strict 5-phase plan (docs -> skills -> CONTEXT.md -> RAG reindex -> .mcp.json restore), all schema + phase-language validated.

Phase 1 (CODEREF-CORE docs) executed + committed c9f844c, pushed 4cd33af..c9f844c on main. 8 tasks: P1-T1 verified all stale-claim locations vs close 4cd33af (valid_edge_count=5154, header_missing_count=7, 11 tools); P1-T2 removed --no-enrich x3 from docs/CLI.md; P1-T3 applied GAP-01 (baseline in-metric 3464->5154, NOT edgeCount) + GAP-02 (README 6->11 tools) + foundation-sync + plugin-bullet removals; P1-T4 full rewrite of guide graph+error chapters onto live surface (ADJ-P1T4, operator-approved); P1-T5 regenerated 4 generator docs + hand-corrected 4 (API/ARCHITECTURE/COMPONENTS/SCHEMA off deleted AnalyzerService/QueryExecutor, ADJ-P1T5, operator-approved), validator exit 0; P1-T6 git rm dead example-detector + tombstone README; P1-T7 no-op (zero analyzer-service-constraint stubs exist). STRICT HARD STOP before Phase 2 (ASSISTANT repo). Kaizen KZ-01KWJV5Q filed on 3x task under-scoping.

Pre-execution /discover gaps@medium audited all 20 tasks against current code: 16 accurate, 4 corrections applied to plan.json (GAP-01/02 critical on P1-T3, GAP-03/04 warning on P2-T2/T3). Re-validated: schema VALID + semantic-validator exit 0. No re-author needed; WO stays plan_created.
